Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "northwindtech.com"). Discover: (a) Pam Kowalski's final-warning escalation email to david.chen@moveops.com (CC: victor.huang@northwindtech.com) naming both the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and the Emilia Cruz piano damage, calling out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, and stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue. (b) Victor Huang's security breach email to chloe.vance@moveops.com (CC: catalina.dubois@moveops.com) treating the server lab move as a direct violation crew loaded before the 9:00 AM IT escort arrived and equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage, both explicitly prohibited. (c) Catalina's April 14 commitment email to Pam promising a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week. (d) David Chen's internal escalation email to Elena saying NorthWind is now a save-or-lose situation. (e) Catalina's draft email admitting she's underwater on NorthWind. (f) The original Q1 correspondence: Pam's apartment quality complaint (HVAC in Austin, misrepresented listing in Phoenix), Pam's escalation to David, David's response with $3K goodwill credit offer.
OE 2: Search emails for Raj Patel's server equipment move using search_emails (query: "Raj Patel" or "server lab" or "chain-of-custody"). Discover: (a) Pam Kowalski's original Denver relocation request detailing Raj Patel's server lab (4 rack-mounted servers, ~2,400 lbs, lithium UPS batteries requiring hazmat handling, CISO Victor Huang's security requirements). (b) Victor Huang's formal security requirements email with non-negotiable chain-of-custody log, IT security escort, no warehouse stops, GPS tracking, disk encryption verification. (c) Catalina's acknowledgment to Pam. (d) Victor Huang's April 10 security breach escalation email reporting the crew arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded before the 9:00 AM escort, then equipment was stored overnight at SentinelChain warehouse.
OE 3: Search emails for Emilia Cruz's move and the piano damage using search_emails (query: "Emilia Cruz" or "piano" or "Steinway" or "KeyMove"). Discover: (a) Pam's original request describing Emilia Cruz relocating with partner's Steinway Model B grand piano (7ft, 480 lbs), needing specialty piano movers, climate-controlled transport, and destination tuning, plus a 27-day lease overlap (Chicago lease through May 15, Denver needed by April 18). (b) Marcus Thorne's email flagging the KeyMove bill ($1,200 insurance claim rider) for the Steinway scratch during stairwell extraction. (c) Catalina's draft admitting she's underwater.
OE 4: Search emails for Alejandro's retention model using search_emails (query: "retention model" or "NorthWind retention" or "Alejandro NorthWind"). Discover Alejandro Fuentes' email to Marcus Thorne with the working draft retention model for NorthWind, including: Scenario A (hold current pricing, weak renewal probability), Scenario B (12% Q3 discount tied to full Denver expansion commitment, with preliminary math showing net +$9,380 if full Denver volume lands), and three open problems: (1) piano goodwill credit absorption thins the buffer, (2) NorthWind may insist on a separate security breach concession making 12% insufficient, (3) partial Denver commitment not modeled. The email explicitly states the model is not ready to send externally and is missing a clean view on Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim costs.
OE 5: Search emails for Q1 performance data and NorthWind billing using search_emails (query: "Q1 AM performance" or "Q1 margin analysis" or "Alejandro margin"). Discover: (a) Alejandro's Q1 margin analysis email to David Catalina: $64,800 revenue, 2 deals, 32% average margin, NorthWind deal had $4,200 in unexpected costs (expediting + goodwill credit) dropping effective margin to 28%. (b) David's Q1 AM Performance Scorecard email Catalina: CSAT 5.5/10, 2 escalations, 0% repeat rate, $64,800 revenue.
OE 6: Search Slack for NorthWind-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "chain-of-custody"). Discover: (a) In #operations (C006): Catalina's initial heads-up to Chloe about both Denver moves flagging NorthWind as high-stakes (5/10 CSAT, Pam escalated to David); Chloe's detailed response about Swift handling the hazmat but chain-of-custody protocol being unprecedented; Catalina's invoice breakdown for INV-2026-0412 ($14,700 total); David's commitment to enhanced SLA for NorthWind. (b) In #operations (C006): Chloe's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure crew arrived 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, equipment loaded without escort, then held overnight at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse. (c) In #customer-support (C007): Julian Brooks' note that Emilia Cruz called upset about the scratched Steinway and asked for a same-day callback that was never picked up. (d) In #executive (C004): David's escalation to Elena about Catalina being underwater, Elena's response ("Handle it within the team"). (e) In #executive (C004): David's post about Pam's final-warning escalation, noting NorthWind is in "active save mode" and PathFinder has been mentioned. (f) In #customer-engagement (C002): Discussion about PathFinder pitching in Denver with "tighter chain-of-custody controls" as a wedge; Catalina confirming she'll check if it's touching NorthWind.
OE 7: Search CRM for NorthWind company record and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind" and/or company_id: "company_northwind"). Discover: (a) company_northwind NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL, Manufacturing Tech, 150 employees, Customer type, description: "5 relocations. Had quality/timeline issues. Catalina's account." (b) deal_northwind_won $60,000, "5 Enterprise Relocations (Won)", closedWon, January 2026, "Aggressive 3-week timeline promised by Catalina." (c) deal_northwind_denver_expansion $14,700, "Denver Satellite Office Expansion (2 Relocations)", qualifiedToBuy stage, April 2026, with descriptions of both Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz moves. (d) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 same deal at contractSent stage with more detailed descriptions. Note: The CRM deal stage does NOT reflect the true account risk it still shows contractSent/qualifiedToBuy rather than anything indicating retention risk or active escalation.
OE 8: Search CRM for NorthWind-related contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") and (full_name: "Victor Huang"). Discover: (a) contact_pam_kowalski pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations, company_northwind. (b) contact_victor_huang victor.huang@northwindtech.com, Chief Information Security Officer, company_northwind.
OE 9: Search CRM for NorthWind engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]). Discover: eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context the full account recovery context engagement note detailing: Q1 background (5/10 CSAT, 4/10 communication quality, 5/10 service quality), the Denver expansion as a second-chance opportunity, Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz move details, security requirements, and Catalina's commitment to recovery.
OE 10: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records with filtering. Discover: (a) 5 original Q1 relocations all Completed: Ryan Caldwell (recReloc00000014, Detroit→Chicago, Phase 1, Fatimah), Lisa Petrosky (recReloc00000015, Indianapolis→Chicago, Phase 1, Javier), Derek Nash (recReloc00000016, Minneapolis→Chicago, Phase 1, Blessing), Amira Okonkwo (recReloc00000020, St. Louis→Chicago, Phase 2, Fatimah), Ben Schaefer (recReloc00000021, Milwaukee→Chicago, Phase 2, Javier). (b) Denver relocations: Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver, Chicago→Denver, In Progress, Blessing Okafor, server lab details). Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, Chicago→Denver, In Progress, Blessing Okafor, Steinway piano details). (c) A second Raj Patel audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411, move dates April 10, Fatimah Al-Rashidi, noting movers arrived at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, loaded without waiting). (d) NorthWind client account record (recAcct000000064, tblClientAccts01, 5 relocations, Active status, notes mentioning Pam Kowalski and Q1 issues).
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to NorthWind) or quickbooks_search_customers (criteria for NorthWind). Discover: (a) inv_northwind_01 $60,000, DocNumber INV-2026-003, 5 Enterprise Relocation Packages at $12,000 each, balance $60,000, billed to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com. (b) inv_northwind_denver_02 $14,700, DocNumber INV-2026-0412, 6 line items: Raj Patel standard relocation ($4,500), hazmat handling surcharge ($1,800), chain-of-custody security protocol ($1,200), Emilia Cruz standard relocation ($4,500), specialty piano moving ($2,200), early lease termination negotiation ($500).
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills/costs outstanding using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to KeyMove or NorthWind-related vendors). Discover: BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 $1,200 insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction, from KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), due April 24, 2026.
OE 13: Search Linear for NorthWind-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or "Raj Patel"). Discover: (a) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (number 482, team_operations, priority 1 urgent, assigned to moveops_david_chen, due April 24). Description references: Victor Huang security breach email, Chloe's reconstructed timeline, missing custody log, Julian's Emilia Cruz support note, Catalina's undelivered recovery plan promise, CRM data not reflecting true account risk. (b) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind "DOT Docs Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (number 3, team_operations, state_done, assigned to Chloe Vance, project: project_dot_compliance_audit). Documentation checklist for Raj Patel's hazmat shipment.
OE 14: Read comments on the NorthWind retention ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1"). Discover: (a) Chloe's ops timeline reconstruction (crew arrived 7:00 AM, escort confirmed 9:00 AM, loaded before escort, overnight at SentinelChain warehouse). (b) David's cross-system picture comment noting both Raj Patel (control failure) and Emilia Cruz (care failure), PathFinder being mentioned, CRM not reflecting true severity. (c) Alejandro's comment about his draft retention model (12% Q3 discount tied to Denver commitment, still incomplete, missing Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim cost views).
OE 15: Search Linear for DOT compliance audit and hazmat shipment tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance audit" or project: "project_dot_compliance_audit"). Discover all 4 hazmat shipments under the DOT audit: (a) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium (state_done). (b) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser (state_in_progress, priority 1). (c) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS (state_done). (d) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig (state_done). Plus subtask: issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations URGENT" (state_todo, priority 1). Raj Patel's shipment IS in scope for the DOT audit.
OE 16: Search Slack or emails for Catalina's Q1 performance data and her other active accounts using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Catalina performance" or "Catalina accounts" or "Q1 AM") and search_emails. Discover: (a) From the Q1 AM scorecard (email): Catalina $64,800 revenue, 2 deals won (NorthWind $60K, Prairie Data $4,800), 1 deal lost (Lakeline $78K), 32% average margin, CSAT 5.5/10, 2 escalations, 0% repeat rate. (b) From #executive (C004) Slack: David flagging Catalina's response time issues on Prairie Data while managing NorthWind, and the February conversation about NorthWind response delays. (c) From the Terraform handoff: Catalina accidentally reply-all'd internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital; Elena assigned Terraform to Mina, ordered Catalina to apologize. (d) From CRM engagements and Linear: Catalina manages NorthWind, PivotPoint Labs, and was involved in Canopy Health Q2 wave and Terraform (until reassigned).
OE 17: Update the NorthWind CRM deal to reflect actual risk using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2", dealstage: a stage reflecting risk/retention concern, e.g., "decisionMakerBoughtIn" or another appropriate stage reflecting the account is at risk) and/or crm_create_engagement to add a strategic note. The engagement note should cover: (a) all open issues (Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz piano damage, missed recovery-plan promise), (b) financial exposure including the $14,700 Denver invoice (INV-2026-0412), $1,200 KeyMove vendor claim (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417), and margin erosion from $4,200 in Q1 unexpected costs on the original $60K deal (effective margin dropped to 28%), (c) the retention conversation status (Alejandro's draft model with 12% Q3 discount scenario, not yet decision-ready), (d) PathFinder competitive threat, (e) Q1 CSAT 5/10.
OE 18: Find the retention response ticket and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1"). The comment should include: (a) DOT compliance overlap Raj Patel's shipment is in scope for the DOT audit (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, project_dot_compliance_audit), meaning the chain-of-custody breach has regulatory implications beyond just the client relationship. (b) Q1 history NorthWind's 5/10 CSAT, 4/10 communication quality, 2 Phase 1 apartment failures, Pam's prior escalation to David. (c) Financial exposure $14,700 Denver invoice, $1,200 KeyMove piano damage bill, $4,200 in Q1 expediting + goodwill costs, $60,000 original contract at risk. (d) Alejandro's retention model status (draft only, missing breach/claim cost inputs). (e) The documentation integrity issue Linear ticket is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located, while Airtable still shows In Progress.
OE 19: Search Linear for other open tickets related to these moves chain-of-custody, piano damage, anything logged by Chloe or Julian using linear_list_issues (query: "chain-of-custody" or "piano" or "Emilia Cruz" or "NorthWind" or "KeyMove"). Find any related open tickets. The DOT docs ticket for Raj Patel (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) is already found. Update any found tickets with relevant context using linear_create_comment.
OE 20: Update (flag) both Denver relocation records in Airtable so they show service issues instead of reading as routine, using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). (a) Update recRajPatelChicagoDenver: add notes to Special Requirements flagging the chain-of-custody breach (crew loaded before escort, overnight warehouse storage), Victor Huang's security incident escalation, and DOT audit scope. (b) Update recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver: add notes to Special Requirements flagging the Steinway piano damage during extraction, $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim, and lack of timely support callback.
OE 21: Schedule an emergency account review meeting for April 28 at 9:00 AM (first thing in the morning, 30 minutes) using calendar_add_calendar_event. Title: "NorthWind Emergency Account Review" (or similar). Attendees: elena.rostova@moveops.com (Elena), david.chen@moveops.com (David), catalina.dubois@moveops.com (Catalina), chloe.vance@moveops.com (Chloe), marcus.thorne@moveops.com (Marcus). Duration: 30 minutes. Date: April 28, 2026.
OE 22: Send email from Elena to David using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: ["david.chen@moveops.com"]). Content: (a) David owns the NorthWind relationship directly until further notice. (b) Catalina is limited to operational handoff only. (c) Elena wants a retention proposal before the April 28 meeting with real exposure numbers and a credit offer informed by Alejandro's model.
OE 23: Send email from Elena to Chloe using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"]). Content: Asking Chloe to prepare an ops timeline for the April 28 meeting covering both Denver moves from dispatch through delivery, including where custody and documentation gaps occurred.
OE 24: Post in the #executive Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004"). Content: NorthWind is an active retention case and the April 28 meeting is mandatory. Tag/mention David, Catalina, Chloe, and Marcus (user IDs: moveops_david_chen, moveops_catalina_dubois, moveops_chloe_vance, moveops_marcus_thorne).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search emails for NorthWind-related correspondence using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "northwindtech.com"). Discover: (a) Pam Kowalski's final-warning escalation email to david.chen@moveops.com (CC: victor.huang@northwindtech.com) naming both the Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach and the Emilia Cruz piano damage, calling out the missed April 14 recovery-plan promise, and stating NorthWind is evaluating whether MoveOps can continue. (b) Victor Huang's security breach email to chloe.vance@moveops.com (CC: catalina.dubois@moveops.com) treating the server lab move as a direct violation crew loaded before the 9:00 AM IT escort arrived and equipment was placed in overnight warehouse storage, both explicitly prohibited. (c) Catalina's April 14 commitment email to Pam promising a comprehensive service recovery plan within the week. (d) David Chen's internal escalation email to Elena saying NorthWind is now a save-or-lose situation. (e) Catalina's draft email admitting she's underwater on NorthWind. OE 6: Search Slack for NorthWind-related discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Raj Patel" or "Emilia Cruz" or "chain-of-custody"). Discover: (a) In #operations (C006): Catalina's initial heads-up to Chloe about both Denver moves flagging NorthWind as high-stakes (5/10 CSAT, Pam escalated to David); Chloe's detailed response about Swift handling the hazmat but chain-of-custody protocol being unprecedented; Catalina's invoice breakdown for INV-2026-0412 ($14,700 total); David's commitment to enhanced SLA for NorthWind. (b) In #operations (C006): Chloe's reconstructed timeline of the Raj Patel chain-of-custody failure crew arrived 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, equipment loaded without escort, then held overnight at SentinelChain South San Francisco warehouse. (c) In #customer-support (C007): Julian Brooks' note that Emilia Cruz called upset about the scratched Steinway and asked for a same-day callback that was never picked up. (d) In #executive (C004): David's escalation to Elena about Catalina being underwater, Elena's response ("Handle it within the team"). (e) In #executive (C004): David's post about Pam's final-warning escalation, noting NorthWind is in "active save mode" and PathFinder has been mentioned. (f) In #customer-engagement (C002): Discussion about PathFinder pitching in Denver with "tighter chain-of-custody controls" as a wedge; Catalina confirming she'll check if it's touching NorthWind.
OE 7: Search CRM for NorthWind company record and deals using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "NorthWind" and/or company_id: "company_northwind"). Discover: (a) company_northwind NorthWind Technologies, Chicago IL, Manufacturing Tech, 150 employees, Customer type, description: "5 relocations. Had quality/timeline issues. Catalina's account." (b) deal_northwind_won $60,000, "5 Enterprise Relocations (Won)", closedWon, January 2026, "Aggressive 3-week timeline promised by Catalina." (c) deal_northwind_denver_expansion $14,700, "Denver Satellite Office Expansion (2 Relocations)", qualifiedToBuy stage, April 2026, with descriptions of both Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz moves. (d) deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 same deal at contractSent stage with more detailed descriptions. Note: The CRM deal stage does NOT reflect the true account risk it still shows contractSent/qualifiedToBuy rather than anything indicating retention risk or active escalation.
OE 8: Search CRM for NorthWind-related contacts using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski") and (full_name: "Victor Huang"). Discover: (a) contact_pam_kowalski pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations, company_northwind. (b) contact_victor_huang victor.huang@northwindtech.com, Chief Information Security Officer, company_northwind.
OE 9: Search CRM for NorthWind engagement history using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]). Discover: eng_northwind_denver_recovery_context the full account recovery context engagement note detailing: Q1 background (5/10 CSAT, 4/10 communication quality, 5/10 service quality), the Denver expansion as a second-chance opportunity, Raj Patel and Emilia Cruz move details, security requirements, and Catalina's commitment to recovery.
OE 10: Search Airtable for NorthWind relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies") or airtable_list_records with filtering. (b) Denver relocations: Raj Patel (recRajPatelChicagoDenver, Chicago→Denver, In Progress, Blessing Okafor, server lab details). Emilia Cruz (recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver, Chicago→Denver, In Progress, Blessing Okafor, Steinway piano details). (c) A second Raj Patel audit note record (recRajPatelAuditNote0411, move dates April 10, Fatimah Al-Rashidi, noting movers arrived at 7:00 AM, escort confirmed for 9:00 AM, loaded without waiting). (d) NorthWind client account record (recAcct000000064, tblClientAccts01, 5 relocations, Active status, notes mentioning Pam Kowalski and Q1 issues).
OE 11: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria related to NorthWind) or quickbooks_search_customers (criteria for NorthWind). Discover: (a) inv_northwind_01 $60,000, DocNumber INV-2026-003, 5 Enterprise Relocation Packages at $12,000 each, balance $60,000, billed to pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com. (b) inv_northwind_denver_02 $14,700, DocNumber INV-2026-0412, 6 line items: Raj Patel standard relocation ($4,500), hazmat handling surcharge ($1,800), chain-of-custody security protocol ($1,200), Emilia Cruz standard relocation ($4,500), specialty piano moving ($2,200), early lease termination negotiation ($500).
OE 12: Search QuickBooks for vendor bills/costs outstanding using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to KeyMove or NorthWind-related vendors). Discover: BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417 $1,200 insurance claim rider for Emilia Cruz Steinway piano scratch during stairwell extraction, from KeyMove Specialty Transport (VEND-KEYMOVE-001), due April 24, 2026.
OE 13: Search Linear for NorthWind-related tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or "Raj Patel"). Discover: (a) linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (number 482, team_operations, priority 1 urgent, assigned to moveops_david_chen, due April 24). Description references: Victor Huang security breach email, Chloe's reconstructed timeline, missing custody log, Julian's Emilia Cruz support note, Catalina's undelivered recovery plan promise, CRM data not reflecting true account risk. (b) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind "DOT Docs Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (number 3, team_operations, state_done, assigned to Chloe Vance, project: project_dot_compliance_audit). Documentation checklist for Raj Patel's hazmat shipment.
OE 14: Read comments on the NorthWind retention ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1"). Discover: (a) Chloe's ops timeline reconstruction (crew arrived 7:00 AM, escort confirmed 9:00 AM, loaded before escort, overnight at SentinelChain warehouse). (b) David's cross-system picture comment noting both Raj Patel (control failure) and Emilia Cruz (care failure), PathFinder being mentioned, CRM not reflecting true severity. (c) Alejandro's comment about his draft retention model (12% Q3 discount tied to Denver commitment, still incomplete, missing Raj Patel breach exposure and Emilia Cruz claim cost views).
OE 15: Search Linear for DOT compliance audit and hazmat shipment tickets using linear_list_issues (query: "DOT" or "hazmat" or "compliance audit" or project: "project_dot_compliance_audit"). Discover all 4 hazmat shipments under the DOT audit: (a) issue_dot_docs_zhao_greenstack Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack / Class 9 Lithium (state_done). (b) issue_dot_docs_tanaka_mosaic Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic / Class 3B Laser (state_in_progress, priority 1). (c) issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS (state_done). (d) issue_dot_docs_reyes_greenstack Joaquin Reyes / GreenStack / Oversize Rig (state_done). Plus subtask: issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert "Obtain retroactive DOT placarding certificate from Swift Relocations URGENT" (state_todo, priority 1). Raj Patel's shipment IS in scope for the DOT audit.
OE 16: Search Slack or emails for Catalina's Q1 performance data and her other active accounts using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Catalina performance" or "Catalina accounts" or "Q1 AM") and search_emails. Discover: (a) From the Q1 AM scorecard (email): Catalina $64,800 revenue, 2 deals won (NorthWind $60K, Prairie Data $4,800), 1 deal lost (Lakeline $78K), 32% average margin, CSAT 5.5/10, 2 escalations, 0% repeat rate. (b) From #executive (C004) Slack: David flagging Catalina's response time issues on Prairie Data while managing NorthWind, and the February conversation about NorthWind response delays. (c) From the Terraform handoff: Catalina accidentally reply-all'd internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital; Elena assigned Terraform to Mina, ordered Catalina to apologize. (d) From CRM engagements and Linear: Catalina manages NorthWind, PivotPoint Labs, and was involved in Canopy Health Q2 wave and Terraform (until reassigned).
OE 17: Update the NorthWind CRM deal to reflect actual risk using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2", dealstage: a stage reflecting risk/retention concern, e.g., "decisionMakerBoughtIn" or another appropriate stage reflecting the account is at risk) and/or crm_create_engagement to add a strategic note. The engagement note should cover: (a) all open issues (Raj Patel chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz piano damage, missed recovery-plan promise), (b) financial exposure including the $14,700 Denver invoice (INV-2026-0412), $1,200 KeyMove vendor claim (BILL-KEYMOVE-2026-0417), and margin erosion from $4,200 in Q1 unexpected costs on the original $60K deal (effective margin dropped to 28%), (c) the retention conversation status (Alejandro's draft model with 12% Q3 discount scenario, not yet decision-ready), (d) PathFinder competitive threat, (e) Q1 CSAT 5/10.
OE 18: Find the retention response ticket and add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: "linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1"). The comment should include: (a) DOT compliance overlap Raj Patel's shipment is in scope for the DOT audit (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind, project_dot_compliance_audit), meaning the chain-of-custody breach has regulatory implications beyond just the client relationship. (b) Q1 history NorthWind's 5/10 CSAT, 4/10 communication quality, 2 Phase 1 apartment failures, Pam's prior escalation to David. (c) Financial exposure $14,700 Denver invoice, $1,200 KeyMove piano damage bill, $4,200 in Q1 expediting + goodwill costs, $60,000 original contract at risk. (d) Alejandro's retention model status (draft only, missing breach/claim cost inputs). (e) The documentation integrity issue Linear ticket is marked Done but no signed custody log can be located, while Airtable still shows In Progress.

Find any related open tickets. The DOT docs ticket for Raj Patel (issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind) is already found. Update any found tickets with relevant context using linear_create_comment.
OE 20: Update (flag) both Denver relocation records in Airtable so they show service issues instead of reading as routine, using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblRelocations01). (b) Update recEmiliaCruzChicagoDenver: add notes to Special Requirements flagging the Steinway piano damage during extraction, $1,200 KeyMove insurance claim, and lack of timely support callback.

Duration: 30 minutes. Date: April 28, 2026.

(c) Elena wants a retention proposal before the April 28 meeting with real exposure numbers and a credit offer informed by Alejandro's model.
OE 23: Send email from Elena to Chloe using send_email (sender: elena.rostova@moveops.com, recipients: ["chloe.vance@moveops.com"]). Tag/mention David, Catalina, Chloe, and Marcus (user IDs: moveops_david_chen, moveops_catalina_dubois, moveops_chloe_vance, moveops_marcus_thorne).

30 The model must pass a query related to 'DOT' or 'hazmat' or 'compliance' or 'placarding' (or similar) when calling linear_list_issues to find the DOT compliance audit tickets.
query construction
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'DOT compliance', linear_list_issues with query 'DOT hazmat', and linear_list_projects with query 'DOT' — all related to the DOT compliance audit.
✓Run #2The trajectory shows linear_list_issues called with query 'DOT hazmat' and a separate call with query 'DOT compliance', both directly targeting DOT/hazmat-related terms.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows linear_list_issues was called with queries related to 'DOT' to find the compliance audit tickets, resulting in discovery of the PHMSA audit scope.
✓Run #4The agent called linear_list_issues with query 'DOT compliance' (limit 20) specifically to find DOT compliance audit tickets. This satisfies the requirement for a DOT/hazmat/compliance-related query.
